subject: Update the keystore for resources and traversal
commit_message:
Update the keystore for resources and traversal

to use the aaf generated keystore for our apps

Issue-ID: AAI-1101
Change-Id: I5a5642f86e8bb924f2c01fa685a0493abf500623
Signed-off-by: Kajur, Harish (vk250x) <vk250x@att.com>

entries.message:
Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

@Harish, i think we need the haxproxy pem file, too.

Patch Set 2:

Guys, see this below comment from Mike E.  Is there anyway you can bake this into your docker image?

Mike Elliott
Apr 25 6:02 PM

Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1

Hey Sunil,

There is a size limit wrt how much external configuration can be "mapped" into Kubernetes in 1 helm deployment. We are unfortunately near exceeding that limit right now. This means that large files like the java keystore files, can not be mapped into configmaps. Previously you had a mykey file that was much smaller, the I assume you then loaded into the keystore within the message router container. Can you please take this same approach and just externalize the new version of the mykey file?  I have already had to ask Policy to do the same. 

Longer term, there needs to be mechanism that allows for each component to fetch/exchange certificates with AAF directly. And then update their respective keystores.

Thanks,
Mike.

Patch Set 2:

if these files are the exact same we have typically stored them at a higher level and shared them with the child charts.
If not then I understand having 2 files.

Patch Set 4:

I updated the keystores to one location so both resources and traversal can be shared. I will bake the keystore into the images but since I centralized them do I still need to remove the helm secret for auth? If need be, I will remove them from and the volume.

Patch Set 5: Code-Review+2 Verified+1
